steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a subject-verb agreement question. The subject is "The Proto-Uralic language" — singular. The long appositive phrase "common ancestor of thirty-seven Eurasian languages with similar linguistic properties" interrupts but does not change the subject. The verb in the blank must be singular.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'are,': "are" is a plural present-tense verb; it violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject "The Proto-Uralic language." ✗
  • Choice B — 'were,': "were" is a plural past-tense verb; it also violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — 'have been,': "have been" is a plural present-perfect verb; it violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'is,': "is" is a singular present-tense verb; it correctly agrees with the singular subject "The Proto-Uralic language." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
